calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2026 Text Ruling
TENTATIVE RULING:
Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT MARTIN POPILKA, P1 ENERGY, INC.'s DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

(Complete tentative ruling e-mailed to the parties).

1 - Defendants P1 Energy, Inc. and Martin Popilka's demurrer to the first through sixth, ninth, and tenth causes of action in plaintiff Peter M. Trapp's first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

TENTATIVE RULING:
Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT P1 ENERGY, INC.'s Motion To File Confidential Material Under Seal.

2 - Moving party P1 Energy asks to take off calendar its motion to permanently seal certain material previously lodged conditionally under seal, to wit the unredacted Declaration of Martin Popilka, filed on April 16, 2026, and the unredacted Declaration of Torsten Groß, filed on April 16, 2026. Because the moving party has withdrawn the request for confidential treatment, the court directs the clerk to unseal those declarations.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Confidential Material Under Seal
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Continued To Jun-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT P1 ENERGY, INC.'S MOTION TO FILE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL UNDER SEAL

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to file confidential material under seal is continued for further hearing to June 17, 2026. The moving papers do not contain a declaration or other evidence (as opposed to counsel's assertions) that would enable the court to make the factual findings required by Rule 2.550. Moving party may cure by filing a supplemental declaration no later than one week in advance of the continued hearing date. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine The Law Applicable To All Causes Of Action In Plaintiff Peter M. Trapps First Amended Complaint Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support ***Redacted***
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT P1 ENERGY, INC.'S MOTION TO DETERMINE THE LAW APPLICABLE TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION RAISED IN PLAINTIFF PETER M. TRAPP'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is denied without prejudice on grounds not raised by either party. Generally the court determines what law to apply in the context of a particular motion seeking discrete relief. The parties cite no authority that the court may or should decide an abstract question of law divorced from any other claim for relief. Moving party does not seek any Code of Civil Procedure remedies. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
